steps.title: Trace the marketplace logic
steps.content: Marketplaces arise when groups want resources they do not control. If scholars underestimated the ecological diversity of Maya lands, they would have pictured the regions as producing mostly similar resources, leaving little reason for agricultural trade.

steps.title: Reject unrelated assumptions
steps.content: Choice A shifts to traders from outside Maya regions, not diversity within those regions. Choice B allows significant crop variation, which would support marketplaces. Choice D discusses surplus production rather than whether markets would provide different products. The needed assumption is about access to products unlike those already produced. The answer is C.
